exemplar.title: building an E-commerce website for my client

detailedExemplarData.title: building an E-commerce website for my client

strengths: The e-commerce scenario and client needs are implied through the detailed design artifacts and test plan.
weaknesses: No formal scenario description or documented client consultation.
No explicit product rationale or success criteria stated.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although no formal planning write-up was submitted, there is enough implicit evidence of an intended e-commerce scenario (through the design diagrams and test plan) to award minimal credit. The student needs to provide a clear scenario statement, documented client consultation and a set of measurable success criteria to meet the full band descriptors.

strengths: Built a two-sided system with authentication, inventory management, cart logic, QR-based payment and admin updates, showing substantial complexity.
Effective use of web stack tools (database integration, sessions, AJAX for dynamic updates).
weaknesses: Limited documentation of development tools, frameworks or version control processes.
No external source attribution or detailed explanation of implementation techniques.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The project demonstrates a high level of complexity and appropriate use of tools throughout, with a complete, functional system exceeding standard CRUD applications. Although documentation and source attribution are weak, the overall sophistication and ingenuity justify the maximum mark in Development.

strengths: Comprehensive design overview including site map, use-case diagrams, detailed flowcharts and annotated wire-frames.
Extensive, detailed test plan covering normal and edge-case scenarios across all major functions.
weaknesses: No chronological task log or record of development steps provided to trace the process.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The design overview and test plan both fully meet the highest descriptors â they are detailed, complete and clearly demonstrate the intended solution. The only shortfall is the complete absence of a recorded development log. Overall, this justifies the top mark for Solution Overview.

strengths: Core functions (login, registration, CRUD, cart, payment, order tracking) are implemented and tested, implying full functionality.
Modular design suggests straightforward expansion of features.
weaknesses: No demonstration video was provided to directly verify running functionality.
Extensibility is possible but would require code modifications rather than plug-in modules.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evidence (comprehensive test plan, detailed designs) indicates the product functions well and can be extended. This meets the top band descriptor for Functionality and Extensibility.

weaknesses: No client or adviser feedback collected.
No future recommendations or enhancement proposals.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: There is no evaluation section in the submission. The work contains no systematic judgment against success criteria, no stakeholder feedback, and no forward-looking recommendations, so no marks can be awarded for Evaluation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student supplies a full set of design artifacts: high-level site map, two detailed use-case diagrams (admin & user), multiple flowcharts for every major process, and annotated wire-frames/prototypes for each screen. These clearly outline structure, navigation and data flow, showing a solid â though not flawless â grasp of the overall solution. This aligns with the âcomprehensive design overviewâ descriptor (upper band 3-4).

No explicit rationale is provided that explains why an e-commerce website was chosen or why it best meets the clientâs needs. The design artifacts are present, but justification for product choice is entirely absent.

The design implies the use of a web stack with database integration, sessions, file upload, and possibly JavaScript/AJAX for live badge updates. This indicates several appropriate existing tools/frameworks are employed effectively. However, without explicit discussion of version control, libraries or deployment, the evidence does not reach the highest level of âappropriate and effectiveâ across all tools, so a sound mid-band score is warranted.

A wide-ranging test plan is supplied covering login/logout, registration, CRUD product operations, cart, payment, order tracking, and session handling. Each test includes method and expected outcome; edge cases (empty input, duplicates, invalid formats) are also covered. Minor omissions (no column for actual results) keep it from full 6, but the breadth and detail comfortably meet the âdetailed and completeâ description.
No forward-looking recommendations or enhancement proposals are offered in the extract, so the strand cannot receive credit.

Flow-charts/wire-frames are clearly self-produced, but there is no citation of external inspirations, icons, or frameworks. Explanations of how major techniques (session handling, database schema, security) are implemented are minimal. This corresponds to âsources used but not identified, limited explanationâ, fitting the 1-4 band at the lower end.
There is no chronological record of tasks or logbook evidence. The design section jumps straight into diagrams, so development steps cannot be traced.

Building a two-sided e-commerce system with authentication, inventory management, cart logic, QR-based payment capture and admin status updates demonstrates a moderate degree of complexity and some ingenuity. While the features are standard in industry, they exceed the low-level CRUD examples typical of the lower band. There is no clear evidence of advanced algorithms or novel techniques to justify the top band, so a mid-range score within 5-8 is appropriate.
No set of measurable success criteria is listed. Without clearly stated criteria for later evaluation, the strand cannot be credited.
There is no documentary evidence of feedback from the client or an adviser: no interview notes, screenshots of messages or quotes.

The flow-charts show modular design (products, orders, cart) but do not discuss how additional features (discounts, user profiles) could be added, nor is a layered architecture presented. Expansion appears possible but would require code modification in several areas, matching âexpansion possible but difficultâ.

The submission extract contains no written description of the real-world scenario, no identification of an authentic client, and no record of any consultation. Consequently, there is no evidence on which to award marks for this strand.

Although no demonstration video is provided here, the thorough design and testing tables imply that the core functions (login, product display, cart, admin edits) work. Without direct evidence of full reliability, partial functionality is the safest interpretation, placing the strand at the top of the 1-2 band.
No evaluation section is included, so the student does not systematically judge the final product against success criteria.

annotations.comment: The submission lacks any description of the scenario or evidence of client consultation. The student must include a clear scenario statement and documented consultation notes to meet the planning requirements.
The collection of site maps, useâcase diagrams, flowcharts and annotated wireframes provides a comprehensive design overview that clearly outlines structure, navigation and data flow.
The user and admin useâcase diagrams are clearly drawn and label actors and interactions effectively. They demonstrate solid understanding of functional requirements.
The cart page prototype illustrates item management well, but adding an example of an emptyâcart state would improve completeness.
The cart badge update test validates dynamic UI elements well, showing effective use of AJAX or similar techniques.
The payment and order-placement test plan covers core flows. To enhance, include load tests for concurrent payments and large transaction volumes.
The admin login flowchart is detailed and shows decision branches clearly, reflecting a good grasp of control flow in the authentication process.
The dashboard access flowchart maps each step well, but consider annotating state changes (e.g., error screen) to reflect all possible outcomes.
The product flowchart outlines CRUD operations clearly. To improve, include errorâhandling branches (e.g., validation failures) to show complete process logic.
The âAdd Productâ and âLogoutâ charts are clear, but the flowcharts lack exceptionâhandling paths (e.g., file upload errors). Expand these to reflect real-world usage.
The user login flowchart is well structured and shows clear decision criteria for successful versus failed login attempts.
The registration flowchart covers valid and invalid inputs effectively, illustrating how the system responds to each scenario.
In the product display flowchart, consider labeling UI elements (IDs or class names) so that the design maps unambiguously to the prototype implementation.
The cart and payment flowchart documents core functionality. To deepen completeness, add branches for payment failure and timeouts to show how exceptions are handled.
The highâfidelity prototype of the login and registration pages shows attention to UI detail and consistent styling.
The user registration screen mockup is well detailed. For completeness, comment on responsive behavior or layouts for mobile viewports.
The admin dashboard prototype displays modules and navigation clearly, demonstrating thorough screenâlevel design.
The user dashboard mockup is clear; however, to support extensibility, outline how new modules (e.g., wish lists) could be integrated within this layout.
The login/logout test plan is thorough, covering valid, invalid, and edgeâcase scenarios. This breadth meets the detailed test plan descriptor.
The test plan omits a column for recording actual results. Adding this would enable systematic validation and meet full test-plan criteria.
The customer registration module tests include both typical and invalid-input scenarios, demonstrating thorough coverage of form validation.
The admin product-module test plan covers duplicates and invalid uploads. To extend, add a stress test for bulk product imports.
The admin delete/edit/search tests effectively verify CRUD operations and persistence across reloads.
The cart-functionality tests include quantity updates and persistence. Consider adding tests for preventing negative or zero quantities.
Order-tracking tests verify filtering and status updates effectively, demonstrating a complete user-panel test plan.
Logout functionality tests cover session expiry and post-logout access. To broaden coverage, add tests for simultaneous sessions on multiple devices.
There is no mention of which development tools, frameworks or version-control systems were used. Document tools and justify choices to demonstrate effective tool usage.
No evaluation section is present to judge the final product against success criteria, gather client feedback or propose future enhancements. Include a full evaluation to meet criterion.
